named_technique: Multiplicative σ + geometric series + odd-prime pair lower bound (3,5) -> sigma(n)/n < 15/8
seminal_paper_arxiv: Lehmer 1900 (Bull. AMS): every k-multiperfect with k>=3 has omega>=3 distinct primes. Mathlib references: isMultiplicative_sigma in NumberTheory/ArithmeticFunction.lean, sigma_one_apply_prime_pow, IsPrimePow.deficient and Prime.deficient_pow in NumberTheory/FactorisationProperties.lean, Nat.geomSum_eq for closed form on prime powers.
bridge_lemma: Step 1: hn + hodd + hw split into ω(n)=1 (n is odd prime power, slot 1327's IsPrimePow.deficient gives Deficient n) or ω(n)=2 (n = p^a*q^b for odd primes p<q, smallest pair (3,5)). Step 2: isMultiplicative_sigma decomposes sigma(n) = sigma(p^a)*sigma(q^b); sigma(p^a) = (p^(a+1)-1)/(p-1) < p*p^a/(p-1) strictly via Nat.geomSum_eq; product < (p/(p-1))*(q/(q-1))*n <= (3/2)*(5/4)*n = (15/8)*n < 2n. Step 3: sigma(n) < 2n contradicts k>=2 in (Nat.divisors n).sum id = k*n via Nat.sum_divisors_eq_sum_properDivisors_add_self and Nat.Deficient.
informal_proof_outline: Theorem: for n>1, Odd n, (Nat.primeFactors n).card <= 2, there is no k>=2 with (Nat.divisors n).sum id = k*n. Proof. Case split on (Nat.primeFactors n).card in {1, 2} (since hn:1<n forces card>=1). Case card=1: n=p^a for an odd prime p>=3 (hodd excludes 2). Slot 1327 already showed: IsPrimePow n -> Deficient n -> sigma(n) < 2n. So no k>=2 works. Case card=2: n = p^a * q^b with p<q distinct odd primes, p>=3, q>=5. By isMultiplicative_sigma + coprime (p^a)(q^b), sigma(n) = sigma(p^a)*sigma(q^b). Using Nat.geomSum_eq, sigma(p^a) = (p^(a+1)-1)/(p-1) and STRICTLY less than p*p^a/(p-1) since the geometric sum from j=0 to a is at most (p^(a+1))/(p-1). Numerically: sigma(p^a)/p^a < p/(p-1). Product: sigma(n)/n < (p/(p-1))*(q/(q-1)). Since p>=3 and q>=5, p/(p-1) <= 3/2 and q/(q-1) <= 5/4, so the product is at most (3/2)*(5/4) = 15/8 < 2. Therefore sigma(n) < 2n. By Nat.sum_divisors_eq_sum_properDivisors_add_self and the Nat.Deficient definition, if (Nat.divisors n).sum id = k*n then (k-1)*n = sum of proper divisors < n, forcing k<2, contradicting hypothesis. The genuinely new content beyond slot 1327 is the ω=2 case via product of multiplicative geometric bounds.
candidate_lemmas: Helper one: case split (Nat.primeFactors n).card in {1, 2}. From hn:1<n we have Nat.primeFactors n nonempty so card>=1; combined with hw:card<=2 we get card in {1,2}. Standard Finset.card induction or direct case analysis on n.factorization support.
Helper two: ω=1 sub-case is exactly slot 1327. Use Nat.factorization_prod_pow_eq_self + IsPrimePow.deficient to conclude Nat.Deficient n and thus no multiperfect (same 3-step proof as slot 1327).

Helper three: ω=2 sub-case bound. For n=p^a*q^b with p,q distinct odd primes (p>=3, q>=5), prove sigma(n)<2n. Use Nat.Coprime.pow + isMultiplicative_sigma.map_mul_of_coprime to split sigma(n) = sigma(p^a)*sigma(q^b); use sigma_one_apply_prime_pow / Nat.geomSum_eq for closed form; multiply through to derive sigma(p^a)*sigma(q^b)*8 < 15*p^a*q^b, hence sigma(n)*8 < 16*n, i.e., sigma(n)<2n.

Helper four: ω=2 + Odd => smallest prime>=3 and second prime>=5. From hodd, 2 ∉ Nat.primeFactors n. Hence min element of primeFactors n >= 3 and second-min >= 5. Use Finset.min'/max' on the 2-element finset.
Helper five: deficient excludes k>=2. (As in slot 1327.) sigma(n)<2n means (k-1)*n < n when k>=2, contradiction. Uses Nat.sum_divisors_eq_sum_properDivisors_add_self.
honest_disclaimer: MEGA-5 extension of slot 1327. Slot 1327 (UUID b4069fdc, status: submitted compiled clean per ARISTOTLE_SUMMARY.md May 31 2026) formalized the ω(n)=1 universal case via IsPrimePow.deficient (a 3-step proof). The present theorem extends to ω(n)<=2 under an Odd n hypothesis. The ω=1 sub-case reduces directly to slot 1327's argument. The genuinely new content is the ω=2 sub-case, which requires combining isMultiplicative_sigma with a numerical bound (p/(p-1))*(q/(q-1)) <= 15/8 < 2 valid for all distinct odd primes p<q (smallest pair (3,5)). Mathematical novelty: Lehmer (1900) established that every k-perfect with k>=3 has omega(n)>=3, but the formalization of even the smallest case (omega=2 odd, ANY k>=2) is missing from Mathlib (only Nat.Perfect/Nat.Deficient/Nat.Abundant exist, no Multiperfect predicate). Euclid-Euler theorem (`even_and_perfect_iff`) exists in `Archive/Wiedijk100Theorems/PerfectNumbers.lean` but is NOT imported by plain `import Mathlib` (verified via grep on .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib.lean), so this target cannot leverage it. paired_llm_consultations: 6 rounds (Round 1 Codex statement formulation; Round 2 Grok-4 strong-version critique recommending odd-only stronger form; Round 3 Grok Lean signature pick A3; Round 4 Grok adversarial check identifying odd ω=2 NO MULTIPERFECT statement strictly stronger than k>=3 ruling; Round 5 Grok final refinement merging slot 1327's odd case with the new ω=2 case as ω<=2; Round 6 Grok final polish confirming statement). Confidence Aristotle resolves: 8/10. real_closure_candidate=false (this is sub_claim_closure on Lehmer's bound, formalizing the smallest case omega=2 odd). Plausibility of compile: ~0.85 (the ω=1 case is slot 1327's proof verbatim; the ω=2 case requires Aristotle to combine multiplicativity + geometric bound, harder than slot 1327 but elementary). Plausibility of advancement beyond prior work: ~0.95 (the ω=2 odd case is genuinely new and not subsumed by slot 1327 or any other prior submission).
